scenario_name: Scenario 11 — Queue Backpressure and Consumer Lag
justification: All pass criteria met: throughput gap clearly identified (300/min vs 15-20/min with ~280/min deficit calculated), unbounded backlog consequence described (16,000+ messages within 1 hour), visibility timeout double-processing risk identified as guaranteed steady-state issue (10×3s=30s matching timeout), missing DLQ identified as blocker, queue depth metric gap identified as primary blind spot, autoscaling recommended (15-20 worker instances), verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Throughput math is precise: calculates 15:1 capacity deficit and projects 16,000 message backlog within 1 hour
Visibility timeout analysis is exceptional — identifies that duplicate processing is guaranteed in steady state, not an edge case
DLQ absence correctly framed as blocker with concrete recommendation (maxReceiveCount=3)
Queue depth metric gap identified as the primary operational blind spot
Goes beyond rubric requirements with additional failure modes: downstream storage failure, OOM on large images, missing circuit breakers
Escalations are well-routed to appropriate agents (MJ for capacity architecture, Shaq for implementation gaps)
Health check critique is sharp — correctly notes it returns 200 even during catastrophic backlog growth
